## Overview

Grafana Labs is the recommended observability-backend vendor for ArcKit as a Service Project 001. Its managed Grafana Cloud offering (UK / EU residency tier) hosts Loki (logs), Mimir (metrics), Tempo (traces), and Grafana Dashboards / Alerting / SLO / OnCall. Critically, the same Grafana / Loki / Tempo / Mimir stack is available open-source, which makes it the only major observability-backend vendor with a clean Project 002 sovereign-profile reuse story — same dashboards, same query language (LogQL, PromQL, TraceQL), same OpenTelemetry instrumentation, just a different operator.

## Products & Services

- **Grafana Cloud Pro / Advanced**: managed observability with UK / EU residency tiers; OpenTelemetry-native ingestion endpoint.
- **Grafana**: open-source dashboard / visualisation tool (the open core).
- **Loki**: open-source log aggregation; LogQL query language.
- **Mimir**: open-source horizontally-scalable Prometheus-compatible metrics store.
- **Tempo**: open-source distributed tracing backend.
- **Grafana SLO**: SLO / error-budget tooling.
- **Grafana OnCall**: incident management / alert routing.
- **Grafana Faro**: real-user monitoring (RUM).

## Pricing Model

Usage-based:

- **Active series** (metrics) — per-1k-active-series / month.
- **Logs ingested** — per-GB / month.
- **Traces ingested** — per-GB / month.
- **Free tier**: 10k active series, 50 GB logs/traces, 50k OnCall messages/month — intentionally generous.
- **Pro tier**: ~£20–30k/year per cell at GA + 6 months scale; reserved-capacity discounts on commit.
- 4 cells by Year-3: ~£80–120k/year, ~£0.25M / 3-year — comfortably inside the SOBC observability budget.

## UK Government Presence

- G-Cloud listed: yes (Cloud Software lot).
- DOS listed: limited.
- UK data centres: yes — UK / EU residency tier with named-region pinning at provisioning.

## Strengths

- OpenTelemetry-native; ADR-005 instrumentation portable without vendor-specific re-instrumentation.
- UK / EU residency at the data-plane layer; sub-processor inventory tractable.
- The same Grafana / Loki / Tempo / Mimir stack is available open-source — Project 002 sovereign reuse with no UI / query-language changes.
- Pricing model is the most transparent in the observability market (active-series + per-GB).
- SLO + alerting workflows (Grafana SLO, Grafana OnCall) are mature.

## Weaknesses

- Grafana SIEM is less mature than Microsoft Sentinel or Elastic Cloud SIEM — pair with Sentinel for the SIEM layer.
- Active-series accounting can be surprising at scale — cardinality discipline required (tenant_id × per-route × per-instance can blow up costs).
- Smaller UK public-sector reference base than the hyperscaler observability stacks.

## Projects Referenced In

- ArcKit as a Service Project 001 (managed SaaS) — primary observability backend in `ARC-001-RSCH-v1.0.md` Category 3.
- ArcKit as a Service Project 002 (sovereign deployment) — referenced as the source pattern for the open-source Grafana / Loki / Tempo / Mimir self-host stack used in sovereign mode.
